summary: I came to Lakeland, Florida in 1940 and have been cutting lawns here ever since. I live on a street facing Washington Park in a small house that used to be a garage. Lakeland is where I make my living cutting lawns for mostly white homeowners, and I often start work before sunrise to get jobs done. It’s a place where I’ve raised my family and worked hard all my life.

I was born and raised in Georgia, and I lived there before moving to Lakeland. I married my first wife in Georgia in 1906, and we lived in Laurens County near Walker’s Crossing. Some of my children still live in Georgia, and I have family ties there. Georgia is where I started my life and family before coming to Florida.

My son lives near Sarasota, Florida, and I keep in touch with him. Sarasota is a place where some of my family members have settled. I remember visiting or hearing about Sarasota as part of my family’s spread across the country. It’s one of the places connected to my children’s lives.

Two of my children, Lazarus and Leola, live in Chicago, Illinois. Chicago is where they have made their homes and work. I am proud that my family has spread out to places like Chicago, even though I stayed mostly in the South. It’s a city I hear about often because of my children’s lives there.

Leola, one of my children, lives in New Jersey. It’s another place where my family has settled away from Georgia and Florida. I don’t get to visit often, but I know my family is spread out across the country, including New Jersey. It’s part of the wider story of my children’s lives.

Laurens County, Georgia is where I lived with my first wife and raised some of my children. We lived near Walker’s Crossing in that county, and it was home during my early years. It’s where my family roots are, and I often think about those days there before moving to Florida.

I have lived my whole life in the United States, moving from Georgia to Florida and seeing my family spread to places like Chicago and New Jersey. The country has changed a lot over the years, but I have worked hard cutting lawns to make a living. I believe in faith and the strength given by the Lord to get through hard times here in America.

I have been attending Ware Street Baptist Church on Fourth Avenue for many years. I have a good teacher there named Mr. Williams, and the church is an important part of my life and faith. It’s where I find strength and community, and I believe deeply in the Lord as taught there. The church helps me keep going through all the struggles I’ve faced.

I saved up to buy a Chevrolet car, which was a big deal for me. It cost about thirty dollars, and I paid cash for it, which was hard to do. That car helped me get around and was a symbol of the hard work I put into cutting lawns. Owning a Chevrolet was a proud moment in my life.
